items.prompt:
A class discussion presents a digital sharing or messaging case.

Message draft
```
Can u send me the quiz answers before class?
```
Show how the learner would identify a safe or respectful action in a simple digital scenario.
items.expected_evidence: identify a safe or respectful action in a simple digital scenario
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates identify a safe or respectful action in a simple digital scenario with bounded and technically valid computing reasoning.

items.prompt:
A learner must decide how to credit a source or communicate to an audience.

Slide footer
```
Photo of eclipse copied from the web with no source listed
```
Show how they would describe one reason a digital action has consequences.
items.expected_evidence: describe one reason a digital action has consequences
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates describe one reason a digital action has consequences with bounded and technically valid computing reasoning.

items.prompt:
A school technology policy case raises questions about privacy or governance.

Policy brief
```
District wants hallway cameras to use face recognition for attendance
```
Show how they would distinguish private, shared, and public digital behavior in bounded cases.
items.expected_evidence: distinguish private, shared, and public digital behavior in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates distinguish private, shared, and public digital behavior in bounded cases with bounded and technically valid computing reasoning.

items.prompt: Why can a digital action still matter even if it happens on a screen?
items.expected_evidence: because digital actions can affect other people, information, devices, or shared spaces even if the action is not physical in the same way.
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to because digital actions can affect other people, information, devices, or shared spaces even if the action is not physical in the same way.

items.prompt: How is something private different from something shared online?
items.expected_evidence: private information should stay limited to the right people, while shared information is intentionally available to others in a bounded group or context.
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to private information should stay limited to the right people, while shared information is intentionally available to others in a bounded group or context.

items.prompt:
In a digital communication or computing-impact case, a learner responds in a way that suggests this breakdown: learner treats digital actions as if they do not affect other people or the system.

Case artifact
```
A student reposts a classmate's image without credit and says 'it was already online'
```
Diagnose the ethical or participation mistake. Focus on digital-actions-have-no-consequence belief.
items.expected_evidence: identify a safe or respectful action in a simple digital scenario
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward identify a safe or respectful action in a simple digital scenario.

items.prompt:
In a digital communication or computing-impact case, a learner responds in a way that suggests this breakdown: learner cannot distinguish what should stay private from what may be shared.

Case artifact
```
A student reposts a classmate's image without credit and says 'it was already online'
```
Diagnose the ethical or participation mistake. Focus on private-shared-public confusion.
items.expected_evidence: describe one reason a digital action has consequences
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward describe one reason a digital action has consequences.

items.prompt:
A digital citizenship idea learned in classroom chat is now presented in a public video comment thread.

Comment thread
```
First comment: 'This app is trash.'
Reply box is open.
```
Explain how the learner would distinguish private, shared, and public digital behavior in bounded cases.
items.expected_evidence: distinguish private, shared, and public digital behavior in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves distinguish private, shared, and public digital behavior in bounded cases in a changed computing context or representation.

items.prompt:
A learner must move from a personal-use case to a broader public-system case.

Public-system case
```
A city app ranks neighborhoods for service priority using reported complaints
```
Show how they would follow classroom technology routines without avoidable harm or disruption.
items.expected_evidence: follow classroom technology routines without avoidable harm or disruption
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves follow classroom technology routines without avoidable harm or disruption in a changed computing context or representation.

items.prompt:
After a delay, the learner is given a new communication, attribution, impact, or governance case and must justify the responsible choice.

Delayed case
```
Student reposts a classmate's artwork to a shared channel without naming the artist
```
Show how they would identify a safe or respectful action in a simple digital scenario.
items.expected_evidence: identify a safe or respectful action in a simple digital scenario
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates identify a safe or respectful action in a simple digital scenario on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt:
Probe digital tool navigation and interface cause-effect through this short audience, impact, or systems artifact before closing responsible participation in digital spaces.

Probe artifact
```
A shared class document is visible to 'anyone with the link'
```
Decide whether the current issue is really upstream.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether digital tool navigation and interface cause-effect is secure enough to support responsible participation in digital spaces
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in digital tool navigation and interface cause-effect from a current-node issue in responsible participation in digital spaces.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: digital tool navigation and interface cause-effect
